mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are statistically higher when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns within the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp threshold, or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and real GDP confirms broad-based economic expansion, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of observations when industrial production accelerates, or the signal reverses direction during monetary tightening phases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the consumer price index reflects accelerating inflation that forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 70% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% threshold, or the signal fails to predict direction in a walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates robust labor market expansion and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal correlates negatively with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year sample, or Rank IC falls below -0.02 consistently.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with increasing retail sales indicate strong household balance sheets and durable goods demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined housing and retail signal yields negative 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising unemployment signals a supply-side contraction and weakening economic activity, which triggers negative short-term S&P 500 returns during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of observations when both indicators deteriorate, or the signal loses predictive power outside late-cycle regimes.
